course_description.overview: A cycling granfondo through the UNESCO-designated Langhe, Monferrato, and Roero wine regions in Piedmont, Italy. The event features hilly terrain between Turin and Genoa, positioned early in the cycling season and part of the Specialized Granfondo Series.

key_segments.name: Langhe Hills
key_segments.description: Rolling terrain through UNESCO wine region

key_segments.name: Monferrato
key_segments.description: Hilly landscape characteristic of the region

course_description.difficulty_narrative: The event offers two alternative routes to accommodate different skill levels and preferences. The terrain is characterized by rolling hills typical of the Piedmont wine regions, making it suitable as preparation for larger gran fondos later in the season.

logistics.getting_there: Located in Bra, Piedmont, Italy, positioned between Turin and Genoa. Start location in Pollenzo, Bra CN.
logistics.accommodation: Available in the picturesque town of Pollenzo and surrounding Piedmont region known for wine tourism.
logistics.registration_details: Registration opens October 31, 2026. Two route options available to suit different abilities.
logistics.local_considerations: The event coincides with mid-year holidays for many Europeans, making it convenient for international participation. A gravel variant (Bra Bra Gravel Xperience, 50-70 km) is offered on Saturday, April 27.

final_verdict.one_liner: A well-organized, scenic granfondo through UNESCO wine regions offering excellent early-season preparation with reasonable costs and flexible route options.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Cyclists seeking early-season training in a culturally rich setting; those interested in wine region tourism; riders of varying abilities due to multiple route options; European cyclists taking advantage of mid-year holidays.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes. The combination of impeccable organization, UNESCO scenery, reasonable cost, convenient timing, and two route options makes this an excellent choice for granfondo participation without significant drawbacks.
pro_tips: Register early as the event opens registration October 31, 2026
Consider the gravel variant (April 27) if seeking additional adventure before the main event
Plan accommodation in advance given the mid-year holiday timing and wine region popularity
Use this as preparation for larger gran fondos later in the season
